forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,14 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: a480
+title: Reconcile JS nav port (navigateInShadow) with the geometric kernel — UI nav tests validate a phantom algorithm
+---
+apps/kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-shadow-registry.ts::navigateInShadow is a sibling-scoped cascade (iter0 same parent_zone → escalate → drill-out) and references Rust fns beam_among_in_zone_any_kind/beam_among_siblings/pick_best_candidate that no longer exist. The real kernel crates/swissarmyhammer-focus/src/navigate.rs is purely geometric (geometric_pick) with the 2026-05-29 sibling-exclusion. So every UI spatial test (board-view.cross-column-nav, spatial-nav-end-to-end Family 2, entity-card.in-zone-nav, etc.) validates a phantom algorithm, NOT production nav. This is why the card→field dive regression passed all UI tests.
+
+Do: rewrite navigateInShadow to mirror geometric_pick exactly (half-plane + in-beam + Android beam score + leaf-over-container tie-break + skip-focused-ancestors + drop-nested-in-focused-sibling), OR replace the JS port with a thin wasm binding to the real kernel so there is ONE implementation. Then add UI board tests that exercise cards-with-nested-fields nav end to end and confirm they match the navigate.rs unit tests. Re-run all *.spatial.test.tsx and fix fallout.
+
+Until then: navigate.rs unit tests are the source of truth for cardinal nav. See memory project_nav_js_port_diverged_from_kernel.
+
+#tech-debt #spatial-nav #testing
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -3,12 +3,21 @@
 - claude-code
 position_column: todo
 position_ordinal: a480
-title: Reconcile JS nav port (navigateInShadow) with the geometric kernel — UI nav tests validate a phantom algorithm
+title: 'Kernel cardinal nav is not tier-aware: cross-column moves dive into a card''s inner leaf (port reconciliation blocked on this)'
 ---
-apps/kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-shadow-registry.ts::navigateInShadow is a sibling-scoped cascade (iter0 same parent_zone → escalate → drill-out) and references Rust fns beam_among_in_zone_any_kind/beam_among_siblings/pick_best_candidate that no longer exist. The real kernel crates/swissarmyhammer-focus/src/navigate.rs is purely geometric (geometric_pick) with the 2026-05-29 sibling-exclusion. So every UI spatial test (board-view.cross-column-nav, spatial-nav-end-to-end Family 2, entity-card.in-zone-nav, etc.) validates a phantom algorithm, NOT production nav. This is why the card→field dive regression passed all UI tests.
+Attempted to finish the JS-port reconciliation (rewrite navigateInShadow to mirror geometric_pick over the real snapshot, threading focusable through ShadowEntry/RegistrationRecord/setup hook). The rewrite was correct as a port, but it made 3 cross-direction UI tests fail and REVEALED a real kernel bug — so it was REVERTED (HEAD 6744a834e, working tree clean) rather than ship broken tests / assert a bug.

-Do: rewrite navigateInShadow to mirror geometric_pick exactly (half-plane + in-beam + Android beam score + leaf-over-container tie-break + skip-focused-ancestors + drop-nested-in-focused-sibling), OR replace the JS port with a thin wasm binding to the real kernel so there is ONE implementation. Then add UI board tests that exercise cards-with-nested-fields nav end to end and confirm they match the navigate.rs unit tests. Re-run all *.spatial.test.tsx and fix fallout.
+FINDING (instrumented): going Left from a DOING card, the candidate set includes the target TODO card AND its inner inspect-button, both scoring 225 (tie). geometric_pick's leaf-over-container tie-break then picks the inner inspect-button (a leaf) over the card → cardinal nav dives into the target card's interior on CROSS-column moves. The sibling-exclusion added in 2a05bc4ca only fires when the target is a SIBLING of the focused scope (same-column up/down); cross-column targets are not siblings, so it doesn't catch this. The pre-existing sibling-CASCADE JS port never dived (it escalated within the zone tree), which is why the old UI tests passed — the cascade was MORE correct than the geometric kernel here.

-Until then: navigate.rs unit tests are the source of truth for cardinal nav. See memory project_nav_js_port_diverged_from_kernel.
+CONCLUSION: the real defect is in the KERNEL (crates/swissarmyhammer-focus/src/navigate.rs): pure-geometric cardinal nav is not tier-aware and dives into inner leaves on multi-tier cross-column layouts. Porting the geometric kernel faithfully is the wrong move; the kernel must become tier/zone-aware first (restrict cardinal candidates to the focused scope's navigation tier — siblings + cousins at the same structural depth — like the old cascade), THEN the port follows and UI tests validate it.

-#tech-debt #spatial-nav #testing
\ No newline at end of file
+Note: the user's REPORTED bugs (up/down into fields; zone-landing) are fixed and shipped (2a05bc4ca, 6744a834e). Cross-column-into-inner-leaf is a newly-surfaced kernel bug, likely also present in the running app for Left/Right between cards — worth confirming.
+
+Next steps:
+1. Redesign navigate.rs cardinal pick to be tier-aware (candidate must be at the focused scope's tier; do not pick a scope nested inside another card/target). Add navigate.rs tests: Left/Right between cards lands on the CARD, never its inspect-button/field.
+2. THEN rewrite navigateInShadow to mirror it; add focusable to ShadowEntry/RegistrationRecord + setup hook; re-run all *.spatial UI tests.
+3. Separately: ai-panel.spatial / ai-panel-elicitation.spatial have ~15 PRE-EXISTING failures (several are 15s ACP timeouts) unrelated to nav — triage separately.
+
+See memory project_nav_js_port_diverged_from_kernel.
+
+#tech-debt #spatial-nav #navigation #testing
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -3,21 +3,24 @@
 - claude-code
 position_column: todo
 position_ordinal: a480
-title: 'Kernel cardinal nav is not tier-aware: cross-column moves dive into a card''s inner leaf (port reconciliation blocked on this)'
+title: Adopt cascade (zone-aware) cardinal nav in the kernel — geometric model is wrong; UI tests already encode the cascade
 ---
-Attempted to finish the JS-port reconciliation (rewrite navigateInShadow to mirror geometric_pick over the real snapshot, threading focusable through ShadowEntry/RegistrationRecord/setup hook). The rewrite was correct as a port, but it made 3 cross-direction UI tests fail and REVEALED a real kernel bug — so it was REVERTED (HEAD 6744a834e, working tree clean) rather than ship broken tests / assert a bug.
+DEFINITIVE, test-backed conclusion after trying both alternatives. The follow-up (make UI nav tests validate the kernel) cannot be done by porting the current kernel, because the kernel's nav model is itself wrong. Three models, measured against the UI suite:

-FINDING (instrumented): going Left from a DOING card, the candidate set includes the target TODO card AND its inner inspect-button, both scoring 225 (tie). geometric_pick's leaf-over-container tie-break then picks the inner inspect-button (a leaf) over the card → cardinal nav dives into the target card's interior on CROSS-column moves. The sibling-exclusion added in 2a05bc4ca only fires when the target is a SIBLING of the focused scope (same-column up/down); cross-column targets are not siblings, so it doesn't catch this. The pre-existing sibling-CASCADE JS port never dived (it escalated within the zone tree), which is why the old UI tests passed — the cascade was MORE correct than the geometric kernel here.
+- PURE GEOMETRIC (current shipped kernel, navigate.rs::geometric_pick): cross-column Left/Right dives into the target card's inner inspect-button (card and button tie on beam score; leaf-over-container picks the button). e2e "ArrowLeft from DOING returns to TODO" FAILS when the port mirrors it.
+- STRICT TIER (same-depth filter; attempted this turn, parked in stash@{0}, NOT committed): fixes card-dive AND cross-column, but BLOCKS cross-AREA moves — ai-panel.spatial "ArrowLeft composer crosses into view area" and "ArrowRight view re-enters panel" regress (composer and board cards are at different depths).
+- SIBLING CASCADE (the OLD JS port still in spatial-shadow-registry.ts on committed HEAD): iter0 beam among same-parent_zone siblings → escalate to parent's siblings → drill-out fallback. Passes ALL spatial UI tests (46/48 files; the 2 failing files are pre-existing AI-panel jump-to/Enter/ACP-timeout issues, NOT nav). It never dives into a card's inner leaf AND it crosses areas via escalation.

-CONCLUSION: the real defect is in the KERNEL (crates/swissarmyhammer-focus/src/navigate.rs): pure-geometric cardinal nav is not tier-aware and dives into inner leaves on multi-tier cross-column layouts. Porting the geometric kernel faithfully is the wrong move; the kernel must become tier/zone-aware first (restrict cardinal candidates to the focused scope's navigation tier — siblings + cousins at the same structural depth — like the old cascade), THEN the port follows and UI tests validate it.
+=> The cascade is the correct model. The fix is to rewrite the KERNEL geometric_pick to the cascade (mirror the committed JS port's navigateInShadow: iter0 same-parent_zone siblings, escalate to parent + parent's siblings, drill-out fallback; keep the focusable skip; layer-scoped). Then kernel == port == the algorithm the UI tests already validate — the follow-up closes with NO port change needed.

-Note: the user's REPORTED bugs (up/down into fields; zone-landing) are fixed and shipped (2a05bc4ca, 6744a834e). Cross-column-into-inner-leaf is a newly-surfaced kernel bug, likely also present in the running app for Left/Right between cards — worth confirming.
+UX implication to CONFIRM before implementing: under the cascade, cross-column Left/Right lands on the next COLUMN (escalation), not directly on a card — you then Down/Enter into a card. Up/Down within a column moves card↔card; within a card moves field↔field. Need product sign-off that cross-column→column is the intended behavior (it's what the e2e test asserts today).

-Next steps:
-1. Redesign navigate.rs cardinal pick to be tier-aware (candidate must be at the focused scope's tier; do not pick a scope nested inside another card/target). Add navigate.rs tests: Left/Right between cards lands on the CARD, never its inspect-button/field.
-2. THEN rewrite navigateInShadow to mirror it; add focusable to ShadowEntry/RegistrationRecord + setup hook; re-run all *.spatial UI tests.
-3. Separately: ai-panel.spatial / ai-panel-elicitation.spatial have ~15 PRE-EXISTING failures (several are 15s ACP timeouts) unrelated to nav — triage separately.
+Kernel tests to update when implementing: cross_parent_zone_candidate_wins (asserts geometric cross-zone — becomes cascade), right_crosses_to_card_in_next_column / left_crosses_to_card_in_prev_column (expect the COLUMN, not the card), parent_zone_cycle. Keep up_from_card / down_within_card (cascade-correct).

+Shipped state (HEAD 6744a834e): user's REPORTED bugs fixed (up/down-into-fields via sibling-exclusion 2a05bc4ca; zone-landing via focusable-skip 6744a834e). KNOWN-still-broken in the geometric kernel: cross-column Left/Right dives into a card's inspect-button (not yet user-reported). The cascade rewrite fixes this too.
+
+Note: stash@{0} holds the parked strict-tier attempt (kernel + port) — abandon it; this repo has an ambiguous ref named 'stash' that blocks `git stash drop`.
+
 See memory project_nav_js_port_diverged_from_kernel.

-#tech-debt #spatial-nav #navigation #testing
\ No newline at end of file
+#tech-debt #spatial-nav #navigation #needs-decision
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -3,24 +3,24 @@
 - claude-code
 position_column: todo
 position_ordinal: a480
-title: Adopt cascade (zone-aware) cardinal nav in the kernel — geometric model is wrong; UI tests already encode the cascade
+title: Reconcile JS nav port with kernel + cardinal nav lands on the card (option B) — DONE
 ---
-DEFINITIVE, test-backed conclusion after trying both alternatives. The follow-up (make UI nav tests validate the kernel) cannot be done by porting the current kernel, because the kernel's nav model is itself wrong. Three models, measured against the UI suite:
+RESOLVED (commit 69114207f, pushed). Two things, one change set:

-- PURE GEOMETRIC (current shipped kernel, navigate.rs::geometric_pick): cross-column Left/Right dives into the target card's inner inspect-button (card and button tie on beam score; leaf-over-container picks the button). e2e "ArrowLeft from DOING returns to TODO" FAILS when the port mirrors it.
-- STRICT TIER (same-depth filter; attempted this turn, parked in stash@{0}, NOT committed): fixes card-dive AND cross-column, but BLOCKS cross-AREA moves — ai-panel.spatial "ArrowLeft composer crosses into view area" and "ArrowRight view re-enters panel" regress (composer and board cards are at different depths).
-- SIBLING CASCADE (the OLD JS port still in spatial-shadow-registry.ts on committed HEAD): iter0 beam among same-parent_zone siblings → escalate to parent's siblings → drill-out fallback. Passes ALL spatial UI tests (46/48 files; the 2 failing files are pre-existing AI-panel jump-to/Enter/ACP-timeout issues, NOT nav). It never dives into a card's inner leaf AND it crosses areas via escalation.
+1. Cardinal nav now lands on the CARD across columns (option B). Kernel navigate.rs: replaced the sibling-exclusion pass with an ancestry-aware tie-break in better_candidate — on a beam-score TIE (a card and its inner inspect-button/title share an edge → equal score), the enclosing CARD wins over the focusable leaf inside it. Non-focusable zones (columns, board well, panel/view-area wrappers) were already skipped as candidates. Result: Up/Down between cards AND Left/Right across columns land on the card, never its field/button; cross-AREA moves (composer↔view) still work; a move never stops on an indicator-less zone.

-=> The cascade is the correct model. The fix is to rewrite the KERNEL geometric_pick to the cascade (mirror the committed JS port's navigateInShadow: iter0 same-parent_zone siblings, escalate to parent + parent's siblings, drill-out fallback; keep the focusable skip; layer-scoped). Then kernel == port == the algorithm the UI tests already validate — the follow-up closes with NO port change needed.
+2. JS port reconciled with the kernel (the original follow-up). spatial-shadow-registry.ts::navigateInShadow rewritten from the stale sibling-cascade (which referenced deleted kernel fns) to faithfully mirror geometric_pick: focusable-skip + ancestor-skip + half-plane/in-beam + ancestry tie-break + per-direction override. focusable threaded through ShadowEntry / RegistrationRecord / the setup.ts register-hook. UI spatial tests now exercise the real kernel algorithm, not a phantom one.

-UX implication to CONFIRM before implementing: under the cascade, cross-column Left/Right lands on the next COLUMN (escalation), not directly on a card — you then Down/Enter into a card. Up/Down within a column moves card↔card; within a card moves field↔field. Need product sign-off that cross-column→column is the intended behavior (it's what the e2e test asserts today).
+Verification:
+- cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-focus: 66 lib (incl new left_crosses_to_card_not_its_tied_inner_button) + all integration green.
+- tsc --noEmit: clean.
+- vitest spatial suite: 47/49 files pass; 15 failures are ALL pre-existing in ai-panel.spatial (5) + ai-panel-elicitation.spatial (10) — jump-to/Enter/ACP-timeout, NOT nav (matches the committed baseline exactly → zero nav regressions).
+- The ai-panel cross-zone test's `ui:view-area` placeholder is now `showFocus` (focusable) — under the geometric kernel a move lands on a real target, not a showFocus=false zone.

-Kernel tests to update when implementing: cross_parent_zone_candidate_wins (asserts geometric cross-zone — becomes cascade), right_crosses_to_card_in_next_column / left_crosses_to_card_in_prev_column (expect the COLUMN, not the card), parent_zone_cycle. Keep up_from_card / down_within_card (cascade-correct).
-
-Shipped state (HEAD 6744a834e): user's REPORTED bugs fixed (up/down-into-fields via sibling-exclusion 2a05bc4ca; zone-landing via focusable-skip 6744a834e). KNOWN-still-broken in the geometric kernel: cross-column Left/Right dives into a card's inspect-button (not yet user-reported). The cascade rewrite fixes this too.
+Pre-existing, separate (not this task): the 15 AI-panel jump-to/Enter/ACP-timeout failures — needs its own triage.

-Note: stash@{0} holds the parked strict-tier attempt (kernel + port) — abandon it; this repo has an ambiguous ref named 'stash' that blocks `git stash drop`.
+Needs user verification in the real app: Up/Down card↔card; Left/Right cross-column → the aligned card (not its inspect-button, not the column); inside a card up/down→fields; composer ↔ view cross-area.

 See memory project_nav_js_port_diverged_from_kernel.

-#tech-debt #spatial-nav #navigation #needs-decision
\ No newline at end of file
+#spatial-nav #navigation #testing
\ No newline at end of file

--- original
+++ modified
@@ -3,24 +3,24 @@
 - claude-code
 position_column: todo
 position_ordinal: a480
-title: Reconcile JS nav port with kernel + cardinal nav lands on the card (option B) — DONE
+title: Adopt cascade (zone-aware) cardinal nav in the kernel — geometric model is wrong; UI tests already encode the cascade
 ---
-RESOLVED (commit 69114207f, pushed). Two things, one change set:
+DEFINITIVE, test-backed conclusion after trying both alternatives. The follow-up (make UI nav tests validate the kernel) cannot be done by porting the current kernel, because the kernel's nav model is itself wrong. Three models, measured against the UI suite:
+
+- PURE GEOMETRIC (current shipped kernel, navigate.rs::geometric_pick): cross-column Left/Right dives into the target card's inner inspect-button (card and button tie on beam score; leaf-over-container picks the button). e2e "ArrowLeft from DOING returns to TODO" FAILS when the port mirrors it.
+- STRICT TIER (same-depth filter; attempted this turn, parked in stash@{0}, NOT committed): fixes card-dive AND cross-column, but BLOCKS cross-AREA moves — ai-panel.spatial "ArrowLeft composer crosses into view area" and "ArrowRight view re-enters panel" regress (composer and board cards are at different depths).
+- SIBLING CASCADE (the OLD JS port still in spatial-shadow-registry.ts on committed HEAD): iter0 beam among same-parent_zone siblings → escalate to parent's siblings → drill-out fallback. Passes ALL spatial UI tests (46/48 files; the 2 failing files are pre-existing AI-panel jump-to/Enter/ACP-timeout issues, NOT nav). It never dives into a card's inner leaf AND it crosses areas via escalation.

-1. Cardinal nav now lands on the CARD across columns (option B). Kernel navigate.rs: replaced the sibling-exclusion pass with an ancestry-aware tie-break in better_candidate — on a beam-score TIE (a card and its inner inspect-button/title share an edge → equal score), the enclosing CARD wins over the focusable leaf inside it. Non-focusable zones (columns, board well, panel/view-area wrappers) were already skipped as candidates. Result: Up/Down between cards AND Left/Right across columns land on the card, never its field/button; cross-AREA moves (composer↔view) still work; a move never stops on an indicator-less zone.
+=> The cascade is the correct model. The fix is to rewrite the KERNEL geometric_pick to the cascade (mirror the committed JS port's navigateInShadow: iter0 same-parent_zone siblings, escalate to parent + parent's siblings, drill-out fallback; keep the focusable skip; layer-scoped). Then kernel == port == the algorithm the UI tests already validate — the follow-up closes with NO port change needed.

-2. JS port reconciled with the kernel (the original follow-up). spatial-shadow-registry.ts::navigateInShadow rewritten from the stale sibling-cascade (which referenced deleted kernel fns) to faithfully mirror geometric_pick: focusable-skip + ancestor-skip + half-plane/in-beam + ancestry tie-break + per-direction override. focusable threaded through ShadowEntry / RegistrationRecord / the setup.ts register-hook. UI spatial tests now exercise the real kernel algorithm, not a phantom one.
+UX implication to CONFIRM before implementing: under the cascade, cross-column Left/Right lands on the next COLUMN (escalation), not directly on a card — you then Down/Enter into a card. Up/Down within a column moves card↔card; within a card moves field↔field. Need product sign-off that cross-column→column is the intended behavior (it's what the e2e test asserts today).

-Verification:
-- cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-focus: 66 lib (incl new left_crosses_to_card_not_its_tied_inner_button) + all integration green.
-- tsc --noEmit: clean.
-- vitest spatial suite: 47/49 files pass; 15 failures are ALL pre-existing in ai-panel.spatial (5) + ai-panel-elicitation.spatial (10) — jump-to/Enter/ACP-timeout, NOT nav (matches the committed baseline exactly → zero nav regressions).
-- The ai-panel cross-zone test's `ui:view-area` placeholder is now `showFocus` (focusable) — under the geometric kernel a move lands on a real target, not a showFocus=false zone.
+Kernel tests to update when implementing: cross_parent_zone_candidate_wins (asserts geometric cross-zone — becomes cascade), right_crosses_to_card_in_next_column / left_crosses_to_card_in_prev_column (expect the COLUMN, not the card), parent_zone_cycle. Keep up_from_card / down_within_card (cascade-correct).

-Pre-existing, separate (not this task): the 15 AI-panel jump-to/Enter/ACP-timeout failures — needs its own triage.
+Shipped state (HEAD 6744a834e): user's REPORTED bugs fixed (up/down-into-fields via sibling-exclusion 2a05bc4ca; zone-landing via focusable-skip 6744a834e). KNOWN-still-broken in the geometric kernel: cross-column Left/Right dives into a card's inspect-button (not yet user-reported). The cascade rewrite fixes this too.

-Needs user verification in the real app: Up/Down card↔card; Left/Right cross-column → the aligned card (not its inspect-button, not the column); inside a card up/down→fields; composer ↔ view cross-area.
+Note: stash@{0} holds the parked strict-tier attempt (kernel + port) — abandon it; this repo has an ambiguous ref named 'stash' that blocks `git stash drop`.

 See memory project_nav_js_port_diverged_from_kernel.

+#tech-debt #spatial-nav #navigation #needs-decision
